checks.statement: ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN last_seen_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
checks.statementPreview: ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN last_seen_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
checks.message: ADD COLUMN "last_seen_at" with non-constant DEFAULT: causes table rewrite under ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock. Column is also NOT NULL, requiring an additional constraint step

safeRewrite.description: Add column without default, backfill in batches, then set default
steps: ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS last_seen_at timestamptz;
-- Backfill out-of-band in batches (repeat until 0 rows updated):
--   SELECT ctid FROM users WHERE last_seen_at IS NULL LIMIT 1000 FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED
-- UPDATE users t SET last_seen_at = <fill_value> FROM batch WHERE t.ctid = batch.ctid;
ALTER TABLE users ALTER COLUMN last_seen_at SET DEFAULT <fill_value>;
ALTER TABLE users ADD CONSTRAINT chk_last_seen_at_nn CHECK (last_seen_at IS NOT NULL) NOT VALID;
ALTER TABLE users VALIDATE CONSTRAINT chk_last_seen_at_nn;
ALTER TABLE users ALTER COLUMN last_seen_at SET NOT NULL;
ALTER TABLE users DROP CONSTRAINT chk_last_seen_at_nn;

checks.message: CREATE INDEX "idx_users_last_seen_at" without CONCURRENTLY: acquires SHARE lock, blocking all writes on "users"

safeRewrite.description: Use CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY to allow reads and writes during index build.
steps: CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY IF NOT EXISTS idx_users_last_seen_at ON users (last_seen_at);
-- Note: CONCURRENTLY must run outside a transaction block (disable ORM transaction wrappers)

policyViolations.message: Missing SET lock_timeout: without this, an ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock will queue behind running queries and every new query queues behind it, causing a lock queue death spiral
policyViolations.suggestion: Add SET lock_timeout = '2s'; at the start of the migration

policyViolations.message: Missing SET statement_timeout: long-running operations can block other queries indefinitely
policyViolations.suggestion: Add SET statement_timeout = '5min'; at the start of the migration

policyViolations.message: Missing SET application_name: makes it harder to identify migration locks in pg_stat_activity
Missing SET idle_in_transaction_session_timeout: orphaned connections with open transactions can hold locks indefinitely
